objectives: Punch trees for Thatch and Wood, and pick up rocks for Stone and Flint
Craft a Stone Pick and Stone Hatchet as soon as the engram points allow
Kill Dodos for easy meat and hide — they can't fight back
Build a Campfire and cook Raw Meat before it spoils
Pro tip: pick a spawn beach near a river mouth or cove — flat land plus fresh water makes your first base and taming pen much easier
Craft a Bow — your first real ranged weapon and taming tool
Build Thatch foundations and walls for a starter shelter
Craft a Simple Bed to set a respawn point
Make a Storage Box so loose materials stop cluttering your inventory
Pro tip: put your bed and storage inside walls, not out in the open — a sleeping survivor is an easy meal for a Dilophosaur
Craft Stone Arrows and start hunting small dinos for Hide
Make Sparkpowder (Flint + Stone) — you'll need it for gunpowder and explosives later
Craft a Slingshot as a cheap, low-ammo way to knock out very low-level creatures
Start stockpiling Narcoberries so you're ready the moment Narcotics unlock
At level 6 you'll unlock the Mortar and Pestle — craft one right away, it's the gateway to Narcotics and Cementing Paste
Make Narcotics from Narcoberries and Spoiled Meat so you always have some on hand
Craft a Bola for utility — it won't work on everything, but it's a cheap way to freeze a Dilophosaur or Raptor in place
Pro tip: don't throw away Raw Meat that's about to expire — let it spoil on purpose, since Spoiled Meat is a required Narcotics ingredient
Upgrade to a Wood-tier base — real walls and a door that actually keep predators out
Craft full Hide armor for a solid defense boost over cloth
Tame your first Parasaur — it's non-aggressive and easy to knock out with a Bow, a safe confidence-builder before anything bigger
Scout the coastline and forest edge for resource nodes and a future base spot
Craft a Waterskin so resource runs aren't cut short by thirst
Build a simple Wood taming pen — a small box with a one-tile door and a ramp works for almost anything that isn't a flier
Craft a Cooking Pot and a Preserving Bin so meat and berries stop spoiling on you
Start scouting nearby biomes for Metal nodes
Pro tip: never knock out a creature you're not fully ready to tame — half-finishing a tame just burns narcotics and food for nothing
Tame a Moschops — a strong all-round early gatherer for berries, thatch, hide, and even some metal
Craft a Parachute so cliffs and drops stop being a death sentence
Build a Dinosaur Gateway and Gate for a proper taming pen
Start exploring higher-altitude terrain for Crystal and Silica Pearls
Tame a Trike now, not at level 5 — waiting until you've got a Bow (or Crossbow) and some Narcotics makes for a far safer, cheaper tame than trying it back on day one
Craft a Trike Saddle so your new tank can actually be ridden
Tame an Equus for a fast, low-maintenance land mount
Craft a Compass for navigation
Pro tip: never shoot a Trike in the head — its frill gives it extra protection there, always aim for the body
Tame a Raptor for speed, combat, and scouting
Build a Raptor Saddle
Start collecting Prime Meat from bigger kills — it tames much faster than berries or regular meat
Explore caves for Chitin and Silica Pearls (bring torches or a flashlight attachment)
Build a Smithy — this opens up metal tools and mid-tier weapons
Craft a Refining Forge to turn Metal into Metal Ingots and Oil into Gasoline
Craft a Crossbow — a straight upgrade over the Bow for both combat and taming
Tame a Pteranodon for fast aerial scouting and river crossings
Set up Crop Plots for a food supply that doesn't depend on hunting
Tame a Stegosaurus for strong fiber, berry, and thatch gathering
Craft Metal Pick and Hatchet — a big jump in gathering speed over Stone
Set up irrigation (Water Reservoirs or Intake Pipes) for reliable crop farming
Start taming a Doedicurus for effectively unlimited Stone and Metal later on
Craft Metal Arrows for your Crossbow — solid damage against armored dinos
Tame an Ankylosaurus for fast Metal and Flint farming
Upgrade base sections to Stone for real defense
Craft a Metal Shield to soak hits while fighting or taming
Tame a Sabertooth for fast, reliable Hide farming and a solid mid-game combat mount
Tame a Beelzebufo — its jump gives safe traversal over swamp, and it's one of the best Cementing Paste farmers
Push your base to full Metal tier for real security
Start stockpiling Cementing Paste and Metal Ingots for the crafting stations ahead
Finish taming a Doedicurus if you haven't already — it makes stone and metal runs trivial
Tame a Carbonemys for an early tank that also fishes well
Reinforce your Metal base and start planning turret placements
Start a dedicated taming pen away from your main base for bigger, riskier tames
Tame an Argentavis — real cargo capacity, it can even carry other tames
Tame a Carnotaurus as a base guard dino
Run several Refining Forges at once to keep up with metal demand
Explore underwater areas for Oil and Pearls (bring fur or hide armor if it's cold)
Craft a Longneck Rifle and Tranq Darts — a big step up in range and knockout power for taming
Tame a Sarco for river travel and fast fish and hide farming
Start exploring underwater caves and shipwrecks for loot
Keep breeding stronger metal and gunpowder stockpiles for the crafting stations ahead
Tame a Baryonyx for swamp travel and strong fishing
Explore the Swamp Cave (bring Chitin or Hazard-tier protection)
Tame a Megalodon so open ocean stops being a death trap
Keep a Preserving Bin economy running so meat, berries, and fish don't spoil on long trips
Build a Fabricator (requires a Smithy) — it unlocks the mid-to-late crafting tier
Craft a Pump-Action Shotgun for strong close-range damage
Tame a Rex for meat runs and future boss-fighting muscle
Tame an Arthropluera for safer cave-clearing
Take on the Megapithecus first — of the three main Guardians it has the lowest health and no special status-effect attacks, making it the easiest to learn on
Bring a strong Rex or Therizinosaur squad, ranged weapons for its rock-throw, and a Yutyrannus if you have one
Craft a Sniper Rifle for safer, longer-range hunting and combat
Tame a Therizinosaurus for strong fiber and wood gathering plus serious combat power
Take on the Broodmother next — tougher than the Megapithecus, with torpor-inducing webs and an acid attack to watch for
Tame a Yutyrannus — its courage roar is a major damage and resistance buff for boss fights
Build a Generator and start wiring up an electrical grid
Craft Refrigerators to replace Preserving Bins for large-scale food storage
Craft SCUBA gear for real deep-ocean exploration
Tame a Mosasaurus for ocean dominance and safe deep-sea travel
Build a Vault for secure, fire-resistant storage of your best gear
Craft an Air Conditioner to make egg incubation reliable if you're starting to breed
Tame a Quetzalcoatlus — the Island's biggest flier and a mobile base once saddled
Craft an Industrial Forge and Industrial Grill to speed up smelting and cooking at scale
Save the Dragon for last — its fire breath deals damage as a percentage of max health, so bring a Daeodon for healing and high-armor saddles rather than relying on raw HP
Craft full Flak Armor for serious damage reduction
Tame a Giganotosaurus — the Island's apex predator and a serious late-game combat tame
Start a breeding program: raise babies from your best-stat tames and imprint on them for combat bonuses
Craft Tek engrams as they unlock from boss kills — a Tek Chestpiece or Tek Rifle are good first picks
Clear all three Guardians so the Overseer fight (the gateway to full Tek-tier progression) opens up
Build out a proper Tek base as more Tekgrams unlock from repeated boss kills
Work toward a full Tek or Riot Armor set for survivability
Breed for mutations to push your lines' stats past what wild tames offer
Start gathering a small team (plus Cannons) to tame one of the Island's rare wandering Titanosaurs
Step boss difficulty up to Beta, then Alpha, as your breeding lines and saddles improve
Fill out your Explorer Note collection for the lore and stat bonuses
Keep refining mutations for health and melee on your main boss-fighting species
Build a dedicated underwater Tek base if deep-sea resources matter to you
Take on the Alpha Guardians once your tames and saddles can handle the jump in difficulty
Stockpile Element from boss kills for Tek gear and Tek structures
Keep expanding your base into a fully self-sufficient fortress
Mentor newer tribemates if you're playing on a multiplayer server
Round out your Tek gear — Tek Boots, Tek Helmet, and a Tek Saddle for your best tame
Thoroughly explore every biome on the map for anything you've missed
Keep breeding and imprinting to strengthen your boss-fighting army
Start planning your first Ascension attempt — clearing the Overseer grants permanent character bonuses
Max out your Tek engrams as they become available
Push toward Ascension by clearing the Overseer on Gamma, then Beta, then Alpha
Keep a strong, bred, and imprinted army ready for each Ascension attempt
Start thinking about what a 'finished' base and tame roster looks like for your playstyle
Polish your base into a showcase — defensive, functional, and organized
Build a pen or museum area to show off your best-stat tames
Help newer players learn the mechanics you've mastered
Fill in any remaining Explorer Notes or achievements
Congratulations — you've completed the core Island progression
Keep breeding, mutating, and refining your lines if you want to keep growing
Try a harder difficulty setting or a fresh server run for a new challenge
Reflect on the trip from a beach with a Stone Pick to a Tek-armored survivor commanding Gigas
